objective: Implement the 6-stage autonomous data cleaning pipeline defined in data_cleaning_policy.md, producing a reusable script that runs on Colab A100 and writes per-stage JSON log artifacts.
success_criteria: Stage 1 (corrupt file removal): attempt torchaudio.load on every train file, log and remove failures.
Stage 2 (duplicate detection): hash first 10s of each waveform, remove exact duplicates keeping richest metadata.
Stage 3 (silence trimming): RMS energy in 0.5s windows, trim below -50 dB, flag clips under 1s remaining.
Stage 4 (Silero VAD speech suppression): run Silero VAD, mask segments with speech_prob > 0.7 for > 2s. Default strategy: mask_segment.
Stage 5 (low-SNR flagging): spectral flatness SNR estimate, flag clips below 3 dB, output sample weights.
Stage 6 (clip length normalization): pad clips < 3s, split clips > 300s.
Each stage writes experiment_log/data_cleaning_stage_{N}_{name}.json with required fields from the policy.
If any stage removal_rate exceeds 0.15, the script prints an escalation warning and halts.
The script is runnable as a single Colab cell or as a standalone .py file with Drive paths as arguments.
evidence_requirements: Return a diff summary and the reproduction command.
If Silero VAD cannot be loaded on the target runtime, say so and skip stage 4 with a logged reason.
Do not invent cleaning statistics — all numbers must come from actual execution.
stop_conditions: The dataset path in birdclef2026.json does not exist or is empty.
torchaudio cannot be imported in the runtime.
The cleaning script would require more than 40GB GPU memory.
